questions.question: What is the name of the puppet theatre in the state of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Bommalattam is the puppet theatre form of Tamil Nadu. It combines the techniques of both rod and string puppets. The Bommalattam puppets are the largest, heaviest and the most articulate of all traditional Indian marionettes.

questions.question: The dance form "Kuchipudi" belongs to which state?
questions.explanation: Kuchipudi is a classical dance form of Andhra Pradesh. It derives its name from a village of state. Kuchipudi style of Yakshagaana was conceived by Siddhendra Yogi. The music that accompanies the dance is according to the classical school of Carnatic music. Initially the dance form was practiced by only male dancers. Later on the dance form was opened to female dancers as well.

questions.question: Which is the only state from where two classical dance forms have originated?
questions.explanation: Out of 8 classical dances, 2 dances-namely Kathakali and Mohiniyattam has originated from Kerala. While Kathakali is a blend of dance, music and acting and dramatizes stories, which are mostly adapted from the Indian epics, Mohiniyattam is a classical solo dance. Other states with classical dance forms are Tamil Nadu, Odisha, Andhra Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Manipur, Assam.

questions.question: Bihu folk dance is performed in which state?
questions.explanation: This is performed by people of Assam on Bihu festival. It is performed by both young men and women, and is characterized by brisk dance steps and rapid hand movement. These are mostly performed during harvest seasons.

questions.question: Nautanki comes under what form of performing arts?
questions.explanation: It is a traditional theatre form performed in Uttar Pradesh. The most popular centres of this traditional theatre form are Kanpur, Lucknow and Haathras. Instruments used are Doha, Chaubola, Chhappai, Behar-e-tabeel. Both men and women perform in this. Gulab Bai is a famous personality of this tradition.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a classical language of India?
questions.explanation: Languages accorded the classical language status are Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, odiya, Malayalam and Sanskrit. The criteria for according the status are: i) High antiquity of its early texts/recorded history over a period of 1500-2000 years; (ii) A body of ancient literature/texts, which is considered a valuable heritage by generations of speakers (iii) The literary tradition be original and not borrowed from another speech community; (iv) The classical language and literature being distinct from modern, there may also be a discontinuity between the classical language and its later forms or its offshoots.

questions.question: "Lai Haraoba" festival is celebrated in which state?
questions.explanation: Lai Haraoba festivals celebrated in Manipur, has its roots in the pre-Vaishnavite period. Lai Haraoba is the earliest form of dance which forms the basis of all stylised dances in Manipur. It is performed as a ceremonial offering of song and dance. The principal performers are the maibas and maibis (priests and priestesses) who re-enact the theme of the creation of the world.

questions.question: Which of the following theatre forms is associated with Kerala?
questions.explanation: Mudiyettu is one of the theatre forms of Kerala. Other theatre forms are Koodiyatam and Krishnattam. Mudiyettu, traditional folk theatre form of Kerala is celebrated in the month of Vrischikam (November-December). It is usually performed only in the Kali temples of Kerala, as an oblation to the Goddess. It depicts the triumph of goddess Bhadrakali over the asura Darika. The seven characters in Mudiyettu-Shiva, Narada, Darika, Danavendra, Bhadrakali, Kooli and Koimbidar (Nandikeshvara) are all heavily made-up.

questions.question: Putul Natch is performed in which state?
questions.explanation: It is a rod puppet form in West Bengal. The puppets are carved from wood and follow the various artistic styles of a particular region. It Is also performed in state of Odisha. A bamboo-made hub is tied firmly to the waist of the puppeteer on which the rod holding the puppet is placed.

questions.question: Which among the following musical form has been borrowed from Persia?
questions.explanation: Qawwalis were sung in praise of God. Qawwali was brought from Persia around thirteenth century and Sufis enlisted its services to spread their message. Amir Khusro, a Sufi and an innovator contributed to the evolution of Qawwali. It is a mode of singing rather than a form of composition. In performance Qawwali presents a fascinating, interchanging use of the solo and choral modalities.

questions.question: Which among the following was the first novel of R.K.Narayan?
questions.explanation: Swami and Friends was the first work of R.K.Narayan. It was published in 1935. He has been conferred with Sahitya Akademi Award, Padma Vibushan and other major literary awards.

questions.question: "Beyond Doubt: A dossier on assassination of Gandhi" was written by?
questions.explanation: The book was written by Teesta Setalvad. It is a collection of information from files and documents relating to assassination of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.question: "Courage and Conviction" is an autobiography of who among the following?
questions.explanation: Courage and Conviction is an autobiography of former General V K Singh. It deals about alleged corruption in the Indian government and the role of Indira Gandhi in Operation Bluestar.

questions.question: Famous film Haider is based on which Book?
questions.explanation: The film Haider written by Vishal Bhardwaj is based on the novel Hamlet of William Shakespeare. It is Shakespeare's longest play and revolves around a tragedy.

questions.question: "Abhijnana Shakuntalam" was authored by who among the following?
questions.explanation: It is a Sanskrit play written by Kalidasa. It is based on the character Shakuntala in Mahabharata. The play was the first Indian drama to be translated into English by Sir William Jones. Kalidas was a Classical Sanskrit writer and his plays and poetry are primarily based on the Indian Puranas.

questions.question: Which among the following books are NOT associated with Indian film industry personalities?
questions.explanation: Dare to Go is written by Kiran Bedi. Childhood Days- Satyajit Ray Bonding- Vyjayanthimala And then one day- Nasiruddin Shah Cracking the code- Ayushmann Khurranaa

questions.question: Which among the following book is a sequel of "Scion of Ikshvaku"?
questions.explanation: Sita - Warrior of Mithila is the sequel of Scion of Ikshvaku. Both are written by Amish Trivedi. It is a recreation and reinterpretation of Epic Ramayana.

questions.question: Which among the following book was written by Lhamo Dondrub, the 14th Dalai lama?
questions.explanation: The Art of Living - A Guide to Contentment, Joy and Fulfilment was authored by Dalai lama. The book covers simple teaching of the Dalai Lama about life with photographs of the Tibetan landscape and people, monastic and lay practitioners of Buddhism.

questions.question: Which among the following book is a biography of Kalpana Chawla?
options: The Narrow Road to the Deep North
questions.explanation: Edge of Time was written by Jean Piere Harrison. It Is a biography of Kalpana Chawla. It covers details about her childhood and her career as an astronaut. It also gives details of flight shuttle in which she died. The author is the husband of Chawla.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a work of Chetan Bhagat?
questions.explanation: A Thousand Splendid Suns is a 2007 novel by Afghan-American author Khaled Hosseini. The first theatrical adaptation of the book premiered on February 1, 2017, at the American Conservatory Theater in San Francisco, California.

questions.question: Jnanpith awards are presented by which among the following authority?
questions.explanation: Jnanpith awards are presented annually by Bharatiya Jnanpith a literary and research organization, based in New Delhi. It was founded in the year 1944. It annually publishes hundreds of books in Hindi, both original and translated works and other languages. It also presents the Moortidevi Award.

questions.question: Which of the following award is given in the field of Mathematics?
questions.explanation: The purpose is to award the Abel Prize for outstanding scientific work in the field of mathematics. It was established on 1 January 2002. It is constituted by Government of Norway. The first recipient of the award was Jean Pierre Serre.

questions.question: Who was the first non-Indian to be awarded Bharat Ratna?
questions.explanation: Khan Abdul Gaffer khan was the first non-Indian to be awarded Bharat Ratna in the year 1987. He was followed by Nelson Mandela. He is popularly known as frontier Gandhi. Other than these personalities, Mother Theresa was the only naturalized citizen to be awarded Bharat Ratna. Bharat Ratna was first constituted in the year 1954. The award is conferred in recognition of exceptional service/performance of the highest order. It is the highest civilian award of India. Recipients receive a Sanad signed by the President and a peepal-leaf - shaped medallion.

questions.question: Which of the following instituted Dr. B.C. Roy Award?
options: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
questions.explanation: Dr. B. C. Roy Award was instituted in 1962 in memory of B. C. Roy by Medical Council of India. The Award is given annually in each of the following categories: Statesmanship of the Highest Order in India, Medical man-cum-Statesman, Eminent Medical Person, Eminent person in Philosophy, Eminent person in Science and Eminent person in Arts. It is presented by President of India in New Delhi on July 1, National Doctors' Day.

questions.question: Peabody Award is associated with which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Peabody Awards exist to recognize when storytelling is done well in electronic media. The first set of Peabody Awards was given out in 1941, broadcasting meant radio. Before the decade was over, the scope of the award grew to include television storytelling. It is named after American businessman and philanthropist George Peabody.

questions.question: B.R.Sen Award is presented by?
questions.explanation: The B.R. Sen Award was established by FAO in 1968 to honor the hard work of staff in the field and in recognition of the role of former Director General (1956 - 1967) B.R. Sen, who transformed FAO from a study organization to a development agency. It is presented to field officers who have made an outstanding contribution to the advancement of countries where they are assigned.

questions.question: Which among the following award is presented by International Civil Aviation Organisation?
questions.explanation: The objective of the award to commemorate Dr. Edward Warner's inspiration and service to international civil aviation and to pay tribute to the contribution of outstanding individuals or institutions to the further development of civil aviation. Award consists of a solid gold medal inscribed with the recipient's name, and a certificate of recognition citing the reasons for the Award.

questions.question: Which among the following award is instituted by Ministry of Home Affairs?
questions.explanation: Kabir Award is instituted by Ministry of Home Affairs to promote communal, caste and ethnic harmony by recognizing acts of Physical/moral courage and humanity exhibited by member(s) of one a community, caste or ethnic group in saving the lives and properties of the member(s) of another community, caste or ethnic group.

questions.question: Shilp Guru is associated with which of the following?
questions.explanation: Shilp Guru Awards are being conferred to legendary master craftpersons of handicrafts whose work and dedication have contributed not only to the preservation of rich and diverse craft heritage of the country but also to the resurgence of handicrafts sector. It consists cash prize of Rs.2.00 lakhs, one mounted gold coin, one shawl, and a one Tamrapatra.

questions.question: In which year was Krishi Karman Award instituted?
questions.explanation: Krishi Karman awards were instituted in the year 2010-11 to reward the best performing States in food grains and individual crops of Rice, Wheat, Pulses and Coarse Cereals. Krishi Karman awards are given to Best Performer States for raising country's food grain production. Krishi Karman Awards comprises of three awards for total food grain production and four awards for production of rice, wheat, coarse cereals and pulses - the crops that constitute the food grain.
